claim: The landmark 1994 three-delays model identifies delay in reaching a facility as a major contributor to maternal mortality, with subsequent studies estimating 20-30 percent of maternal deaths involve significant transport delays.

source.title: Too far to walk: maternal mortality in context

method: Reviewed three-delays framework, transport-delay attribution, and subsequent validation studies.
limitations: Original study from 1994 — transport infrastructure has changed substantially.
Delay-attribution varies by context and is difficult to quantify precisely.

claim: The Malaria Atlas Project travel-time surfaces at 1km resolution cover all of Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia, estimating travel time to nearest health facility by motorized and non-motorized transport modes.

source.title: Global maps of travel time to healthcare facilities

method: Reviewed MAP travel-time methodology, validation, and global coverage.
limitations: Travel-time estimates assume optimal road conditions.
1km resolution may miss barriers in complex urban terrain.
